title: Hybrid Retrieval, Score Calibration, and Rank Fusion · Language Clinic: Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluation
summary: A hybrid system adds raw BM25 and cosine scores, gives missing results zero without defining meaning, and wins globally while exact-identifier queries regress. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the weighted fusion evaluation increment in hybrid retrieval and fusion evaluator. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Tune weights, candidate depths, normalization, thresholds, and filters on separated development data and report slice regressions.
Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-hybrid-fusion-3
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-hybrid-fusion-3.
# Case 5798: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def worked_rag_hybrid_fusion_3(lexical_rank: int = 2, dense_rank: int = 5, rank_constant: int = 60, lexical_available: bool = True, dense_available: bool = True, calibrated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "query3dekrow",
        "tenant5798",
        "indexworked35798",
        "prompt3dekrow5798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if rank_constant <= 0 or lexical_rank < 1 or dense_rank < 1:
        return False, 0.0, "rank-input"
    if not lexical_available and not dense_available:
        return False, 0.0, "no-retriever"
    lexical_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + lexical_rank) if lexical_available else 0.0
    dense_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + dense_rank) if dense_available else 0.0
    fused = lexical_score + dense_score
    if not calibrated:
        return False, fused, "fusion-evaluation"
    assert fused > 0.0
    return True, round(fused, 6), "reciprocal-rank-fusion"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-weighted-fusion-evaluation-1pw5ci
# Competency: rag-weighted-fusion-evaluation.
# Case 5092: a public-benefits policy change.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def evidence_rag_weighted_fusion_evaluation_1pw5ci(lexical_rank: int = 2, dense_rank: int = 5, rank_constant: int = 60, lexical_available: bool = True, dense_available: bool = True, calibrated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1pw5ci = "1pw5ci-ic5wp1-5092"
    assert evidence_variant_1pw5ci
    evidence_axes_1pw5ci = (
        "corpus1pw5ci",
        "queryic5wp1",
        "tenant5092",
        "index1pw5ci5092",
        "promptic5wp15092",
        "evaluation50921pw5ci",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1pw5ci)) == 6
    if rank_constant <= 0 or lexical_rank < 1 or dense_rank < 1:
        return False, 0.0, "rank-input"
    if not lexical_available and not dense_available:
        return False, 0.0, "no-retriever"
    lexical_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + lexical_rank) if lexical_available else 0.0
    dense_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + dense_rank) if dense_available else 0.0
    fused = lexical_score + dense_score
    if not calibrated:
        return False, fused, "fusion-evaluation"
    assert fused > 0.0
    return True, round(fused, 6), "reciprocal-rank-fusion"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Tune weights, candidate depths, normalization, thresholds, and filters on separated development data and report slice regressions.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluation model · Theory Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluat…, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluation model · Theory Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluat…, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 4460 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to fuse lexical and dense candidates without comparing uncalibrated raw scores; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Tune weights, candidate depths, normalization, thresholds, and filters on separated development data and report slice regressions.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluation model · Theory Rag Weighted Fusion Evaluat…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Weights selected on the final test set provide unbiased evidence.
